heading: Compensation for rights-of-way for certain reclamation projects
label: 945a Compensation for rights-of-way for certain reclamation projects
text: Notwithstanding the existence of any reservation of right-of-way to the United States for canals under , or any State statute, the Secretary of the Interior shall pay just compensation, including severance damages, to the owners of private land utilized for ditches or canals in connection with any reclamation project, or any unit or any division of a reclamation project, provided the construction of said ditches or canals commenced after , and such compensation shall be paid notwithstanding the execution of any agreements or any judgments entered in any condemnation proceeding, prior to .section 945 of this titleJanuary 1, 1961September 2, 1964
notes: Amendments1966— inserted “to the United States” and “or any State statute,”.Pub. L. 89–624

Effective Date of 1966 Amendment, as added by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 88–561, § 3Pub. L. 89–62480 Stat. 874Oct. 4, 1966“The amendment made by this Act [enacting and amending this section] shall apply to any condemnation action pending in any district court of the United States on the date of enactment of this Act [] and to any such action instituted after that date.”section 945b of this titleOct. 4, 1966
